description: Week 4 practice pack for payment domain - categorization, patterns, and budget advice

assessments.prompt: User spent $45.23 at 'Whole Foods Market' on 2024-01-15. What category should this transaction be assigned to?
User paid $89.99 to 'Amazon.com' with description 'Monthly subscription renewal'. How should this be categorized?
Transaction: $3.75 at 'Starbucks' on morning of workday. What category?
User paid $1,250 to 'Dr. Smith Medical Associates' with insurance claim pending. Category?
Venmo payment of $25 to 'Sarah J.' with note 'dinner split'. How to categorize?
User has 8 transactions at coffee shops over 14 days, averaging $4.50 each, all on weekday mornings. Identify the spending pattern.
assessments.expected_output: Recurring weekday coffee habit - approximately 4 times per week, $18-20/week on coffee

assessments.prompt: User's grocery spending: Week 1: $120, Week 2: $45, Week 3: $130, Week 4: $50. User shops at Costco biweekly ($120-130) and local market weekly ($45-50). Describe this pattern.
assessments.expected_output: Biweekly bulk shopping pattern at Costco (~$125) supplemented by weekly fresh market trips (~$47). Total monthly grocery spending approximately $340.

assessments.prompt: User spends $180/month on dining out (target: $120). Last month: 12 restaurant visits averaging $15 each. Provide actionable budget advice.
assessments.expected_output: You're $60 over your dining budget. To reach your $120 target, reduce restaurant visits from 12 to 8 per month (4 fewer visits). Consider meal prepping or cooking at home 1-2 more times per week.

components.required: meal prep or cook at home

assessments.prompt: User has 5 active subscriptions: Netflix ($15), Spotify ($10), Gym ($45), NYT ($20), Adobe ($35). Total: $125/month. User wants to cut $40. Which should they consider canceling?
assessments.expected_output: To save $40, consider canceling Adobe ($35) + NYT ($20) = $55 saved, or Gym ($45) alone if not using it regularly. Adobe and NYT are less essential than entertainment (Netflix/Spotify) for most users. Ask user about usage frequency to confirm.

components.required: asks about usage or mentions user needs

assessments.prompt: User's monthly income: $4,500. Spending: Rent $1,400, Utilities $150, Groceries $400, Dining $300, Transport $200, Entertainment $250, Shopping $600, Savings $50. Remaining: $150. User wants to save $500/month. Create a realistic savings plan.
assessments.expected_output: Current savings: $50/month ($200 discretionary remaining). To save $500/month, need to cut $450 from spending. Recommended plan: Reduce shopping $300 (from $600 to $300), dining out $100 (from $300 to $200), entertainment $50 (from $250 to $200). This saves $450, bringing total savings to $500/month while maintaining reasonable spending on essentials.

components.required: provides specific dollar cuts per category

usage_notes.purpose: Practice pack for Week 4 continuous learning loop testing
target_skills: Spending pattern recognition across time series
